name: Trenitalia
description: Trenitalia, the primary passenger railway operator in Italy and a subsidiary of the FS Group, manages a vast network of regional, intercity, and high-speed services. Operating under public service contracts for local transport and in the open market for high-speed rail, it provides critical mobility across the entire Italian peninsula.

name: Italo
description: Operated by Italo-NTV, this is Italy's first private high-speed rail service. Competing directly on the state-owned RFI high-speed network, Italo operates a modern fleet of Alstom AGV and Pendolino EVO trains, connecting major Italian cities with a focus on a premium passenger experience and high-frequency schedules.

name: Frecciarossa
description: Frecciarossa is Trenitalia's premier high-speed service, reaching operational speeds of 300 km/h. Utilizing advanced trainsets like the ETR 500 and ETR 1000, it forms the rapid transit backbone of Italy along the Turin-Salerno and Milan-Venice corridors, while also expanding its reach into international markets like France and Spain.

name: Intercity
description: Trenitalia's Intercity network connects medium and large Italian cities not served by the Alta Velocità high-speed lines. Subsidized as a Public Service Obligation (PSO), it ensures territorial continuity across the country, including unique operations like train ferries across the Strait of Messina to reach Sicily.

name: Intercity Notte
description: Operating under a state subsidy contract, Intercity Notte is Italy's domestic sleeper train network. It provides vital overnight links between Northern Italy and the deep south—including Campania, Puglia, Calabria, and Sicily—offering sleeping cars, couchettes, and standard seating for long-haul territorial continuity.

name: Trenord
description: A joint venture between Trenitalia and FNM (Ferrovie Nord Milano), Trenord operates the regional and suburban rail network in Lombardy. It manages one of Europe's busiest commuter systems, including the comprehensive Milan S-Bahn network, regional cross-country routes, and the Malpensa Express airport link.

name: Trenitalia TPER
description: Created in 2020 as a joint venture between Trenitalia (70%) and regional transport provider TPER (30%), this company manages regional railway services across Emilia-Romagna. It operates a highly modernized fleet, predominantly featuring new high-capacity Rock and Pop electric multiple units.

name: SAD
description: SAD operates integrated regional transport in the autonomous bilingual province of South Tyrol. Working alongside Trenitalia and ÖBB, it manages vital Alpine services on the scenic Vinschgau (Val Venosta) and Puster Valley railways, utilizing modern rolling stock tailored for steep gradients and winter conditions.

name: TUA
description: TUA (Trasporto Unico Abruzzese) is the consolidated public transport provider for the Abruzzo region. Its railway division, inheriting the legacy of Ferrovia Adriatico Sangritana, operates regional passenger services connecting the mountainous interior with the Adriatic coast, as well as managing local freight.

name: SBB CFF FFS
description: The Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) is the backbone of Switzerland's world-renowned public transport system. Famous for its precision, national clock-face scheduling (Taktfahrplan), and engineering marvels like the Gotthard Base Tunnel, it seamlessly integrates domestic, international, and high-frequency regional transit.

name: BLS
description: BLS is Switzerland's second-largest standard-gauge railway. In addition to operating the extensive Bern S-Bahn and regional lines in the Emmental and Bernese Oberland, BLS manages the high-speed Lötschberg Base Tunnel and provides crucial car-shuttle transport through the Alps.

name: SOB
description: Schweizerische Südostbahn (SOB) operates regional and long-distance services in central and eastern Switzerland. Known for its highly scenic routes, it runs the popular Voralpen-Express and operates modern Stadler 'Traverso' trains on the Treno Gottardo service over the historic Gotthard mountain railway.

name: SZU
description: The Sihltal Zürich Uetliberg Bahn (SZU) operates two highly specialized commuter lines in the Zurich S-Bahn network. The system features standard-gauge trains on the Sihltal line and a unique, offset-overhead line setup for the steep, specialized Uetliberg mountain railway line.

name: RBS
description: Regionalverkehr Bern-Solothurn (RBS) operates a high-frequency, metre-gauge commuter network radiating from Bern. Operating closely to S-Bahn standards, RBS manages some of the busiest narrow-gauge passenger services in Switzerland, deeply integrated into the capital's urban transit system.
parentCompany: Canton of Bern, City of Bern

name: TPF
description: Transports publics fribourgeois (TPF) manages a multimodal transport network in the canton of Fribourg. Its railway operations encompass both standard and metre-gauge lines, providing crucial connectivity between the bilingual canton's rural communities and urban centers like Bulle and Murten.

name: Zentralbahn
description: A subsidiary of SBB, Zentralbahn operates an extensive metre-gauge network connecting Lucerne with Interlaken and Engelberg. Its routes heavily utilize rack-and-pinion technology to conquer steep Alpine gradients, providing both vital commuter links and world-famous scenic tourist journeys over the Brünig Pass.

name: Appenzeller Bahnen
description: Appenzeller Bahnen operates a network of narrow-gauge railways in the rolling hills of northeastern Switzerland. Serving the Appenzell cantons and St. Gallen, its operations include steep adhesion lines and historic rack railways, deeply embedded in the local Alpine landscape.

name: Thurbo
description: Jointly owned by SBB and the Canton of Thurgau, Thurbo operates an extensive regional railway network in eastern Switzerland. Primarily managing the St. Gallen S-Bahn, its frequent services cross into neighboring Germany and connect numerous communities around the Lake Constance area.

name: TPC
description: Transports Publics du Chablais (TPC) operates four distinct metre-gauge mountain railways originating from Aigle and Bex in the canton of Vaud. Using extensive rack-and-pinion sections, it connects the Rhône Valley to major Alpine resorts like Leysin, Les Diablerets, and Champéry.

name: TMR
description: Transports de Martigny et Régions (TMR) operates two vital railway lines in the Valais Alps: the standard-gauge Saint-Bernard Express heading towards Italy, and the metre-gauge Mont-Blanc Express, which features rack-and-pinion sections and crosses the French border directly to Chamonix.

name: Matterhorn Gotthard Bahn
description: MGB is a renowned metre-gauge railway operating in the high Alps between Zermatt, Andermatt, and Disentis. Utilizing extensive rack railway systems, it provides essential year-round transit for car-free Zermatt and co-operates the legendary Glacier Express in partnership with the RhB.

name: Rhätische Bahn
description: The Rhätische Bahn (RhB) operates the largest narrow-gauge network in Switzerland, covering the mountainous canton of Graubünden. Renowned globally for its engineering marvels, its Albula and Bernina lines—the latter crossing the Alps via adhesion alone without rack assistance—are a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: RegionAlps
description: A joint venture between SBB, TMR, and the Canton of Valais, RegionAlps provides the regional passenger rail service along the Rhône Valley. It connects communities from St-Gingolph on Lake Geneva to Brig, forming the backbone of local transit in the Valais.
parentCompany: SBB CFF FFS / TMR / Canton of Valais

name: MOB
description: Montreux–Oberland Bernois (MOB) operates a scenic metre-gauge railway linking Lake Geneva with the Bernese Oberland. It is internationally famous for the GoldenPass Express, which features innovative variable-gauge trains that seamlessly transition from metre to standard gauge at Zweisimmen.

name: FLP
description: Ferrovie Luganesi (FLP) operates a high-frequency, metre-gauge commuter railway connecting Lugano with Ponte Tresa on the Italian border. Integrated as line S60 of the Ticino S-Bahn, it serves as a critical rapid transit artery for the densely populated Malcantone region.

name: ÖBB
description: The Austrian Federal Railways (ÖBB) operates a comprehensive domestic and international network. It is Europe's leading night train operator through its expansive Nightjet network, while its high-speed Railjet services provide premier daytime connections across Austria, Germany, Switzerland, and Italy.

name: SNCF
description: The French National Railway Company (SNCF) operates one of Europe's largest rail networks. World-renowned for pioneering the TGV high-speed network, it manages everything from rapid international connections (TGV inOui, Ouigo) to extensive regional transit (TER) across France and into neighboring nations.

name: TGV INOUI
description: TGV INOUI is SNCF Voyageurs' flagship high-speed brand, rolled out from 2017 to replace the historic TGV name on its premium services. Running at up to 320 km/h on France's LGV network, it links Paris with major cities nationwide and crosses into Switzerland, Italy, and Germany.

name: OUIGO
description: OUIGO is SNCF's low-cost high-speed brand, launched in 2013 to offer no-frills TGV travel at budget fares. It operates double-deck high-speed trainsets on France's busiest corridors, complemented since 2022 by OUIGO Train Classique conventional-speed services and high-speed operations in Spain.

name: TER
description: TER (Transport express régional) is the brand for France's regional rail services, operated by SNCF Voyageurs under contract with the regional councils that organize and fund them. Each region runs its own livery and sub-brand (Fluo, liO, NOMAD, Rémi, and others) over the shared TER identity.

name: Fluo Grand Est
description: Fluo Grand Est is the regional TER brand for the Grand Est region of north-eastern France. Operated by SNCF Voyageurs under contract with the Grand Est council, it unifies regional trains and buses across Alsace, Lorraine, and Champagne-Ardenne, including cross-border services toward Germany, Luxembourg, and Belgium.

name: TER Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
description: TER Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes is the regional rail brand for the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region in south-eastern France. Operated by SNCF Voyageurs for the regional council, it serves a dense network around Lyon, Grenoble, Saint-Étienne, and Clermont-Ferrand, reaching into the Alps and Massif Central.

name: TER Nouvelle-Aquitaine
description: TER Nouvelle-Aquitaine is the regional rail brand for Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France's largest region by area. Operated by SNCF Voyageurs for the regional council, it connects Bordeaux, Limoges, Poitiers, and Pau across the Atlantic coast, the Pyrenees, and the rural interior.

name: ZOU !
description: ZOU ! is the regional transport brand for the Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur region in south-eastern France. Its TER rail services, operated by SNCF Voyageurs for the regional council, link Marseille, Nice, Toulon, and Avignon along the Mediterranean coast and into the southern Alps.

name: Transilien
description: Transilien is SNCF Voyageurs' suburban rail network for the Île-de-France region around Paris. Operating lines H, J, K, L, N, P, R, and U alongside the SNCF-run portions of the RER, it forms one of Europe's busiest commuter systems under the authority of Île-de-France Mobilités.

name: RER
description: The RER (Réseau Express Régional) is the high-capacity express rail network serving Paris and the Île-de-France region. Its five lines (A–E) combine metro-style frequencies in the city centre with suburban reach, jointly operated by SNCF and RATP under Île-de-France Mobilités.

name: Léman Express
description: Léman Express (LEX) is the cross-border regional rail network centred on Geneva, linking the Swiss canton with neighbouring France since its 2019 launch. Operated by the SNCF–SBB joint venture Lémanis, it is the largest cross-border regional network in Europe, built around the Cornavin–Eaux-Vives–Annemasse (CEVA) line.

name: TGV Lyria
description: TGV Lyria is the high-speed brand for rail travel between France and Switzerland, operated by the SNCF–SBB joint venture Lyria. Running Euroduplex trainsets at up to 320 km/h, it links Paris with Geneva, Lausanne, Basel, Zurich, and the Swiss Alps.

name: VR Group
description: VR Group is Finland's state-owned railway company, operating high-speed Pendolino, InterCity, commuter, and freight services on Finland's 1,524 mm broad-gauge network. Since 2022, VR has expanded heavily into Sweden through VR Sverige (operating regional systems) and the acquisition of the Stockholm–Göteborg high-speed corridor (VR Snabbtåg).

name: SNCB/NMBS
description: The National Railway Company of Belgium (SNCB/NMBS) operates one of the world's most densely spaced railway networks. It provides high-frequency intercity and local services, functioning as a central hub for international transit in Western Europe and managing the comprehensive Brussels RER system.

name: Eurostar
description: Eurostar operates high-speed international rail services connecting the UK with mainland Europe via the Channel Tunnel. Following its merger with Thalys, the unified Eurostar brand now also manages the premier high-speed network linking Paris, Brussels, Amsterdam, and Cologne, creating a unified Western European high-speed giant.

name: Deutsche Bahn
description: Deutsche Bahn (DB) is Germany's national railway operator and a major force in European transit. Its ICE (Intercity-Express) network forms the high-speed backbone of Central Europe, deeply integrated with regular-interval timetables that connect major German hubs with Switzerland, Austria, and beyond.

name: FlixTrain
description: FlixTrain is a low-cost open-access long-distance train operator in Germany, run by FlixMobility. It competes directly with Deutsche Bahn on popular intercity routes, offering affordable fares on services between major cities like Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, and Cologne.

name: Arriva
description: Arriva is a major European transport operator. Acquired by I Squared Capital in 2024, it runs regional rail and bus services across multiple countries, including Arriva Nederland in the Netherlands and the former Arriva Denmark rail business that now operates under the GoCollective name. It also operates transit networks in the UK, Germany, the Czech Republic, Poland, and other European markets.

name: Leo Express
description: Leo Express is a Czech private rail and bus operator focused on modern intercity travel across Central Europe. Its black-and-gold brand is associated with open-access services from Prague toward Ostrava, Slovakia, and Poland, alongside regional public-service operations and connecting coach routes.

name: RegioJet
description: RegioJet is a Czech private rail and coach operator owned by Student Agency. The yellow RegioJet brand is known for open-access long-distance trains in Czechia and Slovakia, with international services linking Central European cities such as Prague, Brno, Vienna, Bratislava, and Polish destinations.

name: DSB
description: DSB is Denmark's state-owned national rail operator and the backbone of passenger rail in the country. It runs the main long-distance and regional network together with Copenhagen's S-train system, carrying roughly 195 million passengers a year across Denmark and key cross-border corridors.

name: Lokaltog
description: Lokaltog is Denmark's largest local railway company, operating local passenger lines across Zealand and Lolland-Falster. The company runs a dense regional network with more than 100 stations and is majority-owned by Movia together with local municipalities and regional stakeholders.

name: NJ
description: Nordjyske Jernbaner (NJ) is the regional rail operator in North Jutland. Founded in 2001 through the merger of Skagensbanen and Hjørring Privatbaner, it runs local passenger services on the Hirtshals-Hjørring and Skagen-Skørping corridors and is majority-owned by Nordjyllands Trafikselskab.

name: Midtjyske Jernbaner
description: Midtjyske Jernbaner is a West Jutland regional railway operator founded in 2008 through the merger of Odderbanen and Lemvigbanen. Today it operates the Vemb-Lemvig-Thyborøn line and the Holstebro-Skjern corridor, with its operations and control center based in Lemvig.

name: Øresundståg
description: Øresundståg is the cross-border regional train system linking Denmark with southern Sweden. The brand began with the opening of the fixed link across the Øresund in 2000 and today connects Copenhagen and Helsingør with Malmö, Gothenburg, Kalmar, Karlskrona, and other cities across southern Sweden.
parentCompany: Öresundståg AB / Southern Swedish regional authorities

name: Skånetrafiken
description: Skånetrafiken is the public transport authority and passenger brand for Sweden's Skåne region. In Rail Radar it is modeled as a Denmark-facing cross-border rail operator, because this brand appears in our Denmark live data for services across the Øresund to Copenhagen.

name: Hessische Landesbahn
description: Hessische Landesbahn (HLB) is a regional rail operator based in Hesse, serving routes across central Germany. Owned by the State of Hesse, it operates RB and RE services connecting Frankfurt, Giessen, Fulda, Siegen, and surrounding areas.

name: ODEG
description: Ostdeutsche Eisenbahn (ODEG) is the largest private regional rail operator in eastern Germany, serving Brandenburg, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, and Berlin. It operates RE and RB lines connecting Berlin with surrounding regions including routes to the Baltic coast and Polish border.

name: Süd-Thüringen-Bahn
description: Süd-Thüringen-Bahn (STB) is a regional rail operator in southern Thuringia, running services on non-electrified lines in the Thuringian Forest region. It connects cities like Erfurt, Meiningen, and Eisenach with smaller towns in the area.

name: Vias Rail
description: Vias Rail is a regional rail operator in Hesse and Rhineland-Palatinate, running RB and RE services in the Rhine-Main area. It operates lines connecting Frankfurt, Mainz, Darmstadt, and Wiesbaden with the surrounding region.

name: WestfalenBahn
description: WestfalenBahn is a regional rail operator in North Rhine-Westphalia and Lower Saxony, running RE and RB services connecting Bielefeld, Osnabrück, Münster, Braunschweig, and Hanover.

name: Vogtlandbahn
description: Vogtlandbahn, operating under the Trilex and Die Länderbahn brands, is a regional rail operator in Saxony and Thuringia. It runs cross-border services into the Czech Republic and connects cities like Zwickau, Plauen, Hof, and Dresden with surrounding regions.

name: NS
description: Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS) operates the principal passenger railway network in the Netherlands. Known for its extremely high-frequency, timetable-less ('Spoorboekloos') operations on major corridors, NS runs a modern, 100% wind-powered electric fleet providing crucial intercity and sprinter services.

name: Great Western Railway
description: Great Western Railway operates intercity and regional services from London Paddington to South West England, South Wales, and the West Midlands. Its network covers some of the UK's most scenic coastal and rural routes.

name: Avanti West Coast
description: Avanti West Coast operates intercity services on the West Coast Main Line, connecting London Euston with Birmingham, Manchester, Liverpool, Glasgow, and Edinburgh. It took over the franchise from Virgin Trains in 2019.

name: LNER
description: London North Eastern Railway operates intercity services on the East Coast Main Line, connecting London King's Cross with Leeds, York, Newcastle, and Edinburgh using its fleet of Azuma trains.

name: CrossCountry
description: CrossCountry operates the most geographically extensive passenger rail franchise in the UK, connecting major cities without passing through London. Its routes link Aberdeen, Edinburgh, and Newcastle with Birmingham, Bristol, Plymouth, and Penzance.

name: ScotRail
description: ScotRail operates the majority of passenger rail services within Scotland, connecting the Central Belt cities with the Highlands, Borders, and rural communities. It was brought under public ownership in 2022.

name: East Midlands Railway
description: East Midlands Railway operates intercity services from London St Pancras to Sheffield, Nottingham, and Derby, as well as regional services across the East Midlands and East Anglia.

name: South Western Railway
description: South Western Railway operates services from London Waterloo to Surrey, Hampshire, Dorset, Devon, Somerset, and Wiltshire. It was brought into public ownership by the Department for Transport in May 2025.

name: Southeastern
description: Southeastern operates commuter and regional services in South East London, Kent, and East Sussex, including high-speed services via HS1 from London St Pancras to Ashford, Canterbury, and the Medway Towns.

name: Elizabeth line
description: The Elizabeth line is a high-frequency cross-London railway connecting Reading and Heathrow in the west with Shenfield and Abbey Wood in the east. Opened in 2022, it is one of Europe's largest infrastructure projects and currently the UK's busiest railway.

name: Southern
description: Southern operates commuter services across South London, Surrey, Sussex, and the South Coast, including services to Brighton, Southampton, and Gatwick Airport. It is part of the Govia Thameslink Railway (GTR) franchise.

name: Northern Trains
description: Northern Trains is the largest train operator in the North of England, running regional and commuter services across Lancashire, Yorkshire, Cumbria, and the North East. It operates over 2,500 services daily and has been under public ownership since 2020.

name: Greater Anglia
description: Greater Anglia operates services from London Liverpool Street to Essex, Suffolk, Norfolk, and Cambridgeshire. It was brought into public ownership by the Department for Transport in October 2025.

name: West Midlands Trains
description: West Midlands Trains operates London Northwestern Railway and West Midlands Railway services, connecting London Euston with Birmingham, Crewe, and Liverpool. It was taken into public ownership by the Department for Transport in February 2026.

name: Thameslink
description: Thameslink operates cross-London services running north-south through the city centre, connecting Bedford and Cambridge with Brighton and Gatwick Airport. It is part of the Govia Thameslink Railway (GTR) franchise.

name: TransPennine Express
description: TransPennine Express operates intercity and express services across the North of England and into Scotland, connecting Liverpool, Manchester, Leeds, York, Newcastle, and Edinburgh. It was brought into public ownership in May 2023.

name: London Overground
description: London Overground is a suburban rail network operated by Transport for London, providing orbital and radial services across Greater London. Its lines were given individual names in 2024: Mildmay, Lioness, Windrush, Weaver, Suffragette, and Liberty.

name: c2c
description: c2c operates commuter services from London Fenchurch Street to Southend and towns across South Essex. It consistently ranks among the most punctual train operators in the UK.

name: Transport for Wales
description: Transport for Wales operates rail services across Wales and the border regions into England, including the South Wales Main Line, Valley Lines commuter network, and the scenic Cambrian and Heart of Wales lines. It took over from Arriva Trains Wales in 2018 under Welsh Government ownership.

name: Chiltern Railways
description: Chiltern Railways operates services from London Marylebone to Birmingham, Aylesbury, and High Wycombe. Known for its consistently high customer satisfaction ratings among UK train operators.

name: Caledonian Sleeper
description: Caledonian Sleeper operates overnight rail services between London Euston and Scotland, serving Edinburgh, Glasgow, Aberdeen, Inverness, and Fort William. It provides a unique overnight travel experience with seated and sleeper berth options.

name: Grand Central
description: Grand Central is an open-access operator running services on the East Coast Main Line, connecting London King's Cross with Sunderland, Hartlepool, and Bradford. It offers an alternative to LNER on the route, serving towns not on the main intercity network.

name: Hull Trains
description: Hull Trains is an open-access operator running direct services between Hull and London King's Cross via Doncaster. It was the UK's first open-access passenger rail operator, providing an alternative to LNER on the East Coast Main Line.

name: Iarnród Éireann
description: Iarnród Éireann (Irish Rail) is Ireland's national railway operator, running intercity, commuter, and DART (Dublin Area Rapid Transit) services across the island. It connects all major Irish cities and operates the electrified DART coastal line serving the greater Dublin area.

name: Vy
description: Vy is Norway's largest passenger rail operator, running the Oslo commuter network, regional services across Eastern Norway, the Bergen Line, and several cross-border routes into Sweden. It is the successor brand to NSB and forms the core of the Norwegian passenger rail market.

name: SJ NORD
description: SJ NORD operates Norway's northern traffic package, including the Dovre Line, Nordland Line, Raumabanen, Roros Line, Tronder commuter rail, and related regional services. It is the Norwegian arm of SJ AB and a major long-distance operator outside the Oslo-centered Vy network.

name: Go-Ahead Norge
description: Go-Ahead Norge operates the southern traffic package, centered on the Sorland Line, Jaeren Line, and Arendal Line between Oslo, Kristiansand, Stavanger, and nearby commuter corridors. It entered the Norwegian market as the country's first competitively tendered long-distance rail operator.

name: Flytoget
description: Flytoget is Norway's dedicated airport express brand, linking Oslo city stations with Oslo Airport at high frequency using fast electric multiple units. It is one of the most recognizable rail brands in the country and focuses on premium airport access.

name: SJ
description: SJ AB is Sweden's state-owned national rail operator, running high-speed, intercity, regional, commuter, and night-train services across the country. SJ operates the tilting X 2000 and SJ 3000 long-distance trains, and since 2024 also runs Stockholm commuter rail (Stockholmståg). Its subsidiaries include SJ Götalandståg, SJ Norrlandståg, and SJ Öresundståg.

name: Arlanda Express
description: Arlanda Express is Sweden's dedicated airport rail link between Stockholm Central Station and Stockholm Arlanda Airport. Its high-frequency electric trains provide a fast, direct connection between the capital and Sweden's largest airport.

name: Mälartåg
description: Mälartåg is the regional rail brand for the Mälaren Valley and surrounding counties in central Sweden. The network connects Stockholm with cities including Uppsala, Västerås, Örebro, Eskilstuna, Norrköping, and Linköping under regional public transport cooperation.

name: Västtrafik
description: Västtrafik is the public transport brand for Västra Götaland in western Sweden. Its rail services connect Göteborg with regional cities and commuter corridors across the county, including routes toward Alingsås, Borås, Uddevalla, Skövde, Vänersborg, and Strömstad.

name: MTR Express
description: MTR Express was Sweden's first private open-access intercity rail operator, running frequent services between Stockholm and Göteborg. Originally a subsidiary of Hong Kong's MTR Corporation, it was acquired by VR Group in 2024 and rebranded as VR Snabbtåg.

name: Snälltåget
description: Snälltåget is a Swedish open-access long-distance rail operator running intercity and night-train services, including seasonal routes to Berlin and ski trains to Åre and Swedish Lapland. It is owned by the French transport group Transdev.

name: Transdev
description: Transdev is a global public transport operator headquartered in France, running rail, bus, tram, and ferry services across 17+ countries. In Sweden, Transdev operates regional rail services and the Snälltåget long-distance brand through its subsidiary Transdev Sverige AB.

name: PKP Intercity
description: PKP Intercity is Poland's national long-distance passenger railway operator and part of the PKP Group. It runs the country's main intercity network under brands such as TLK, IC, EIC, and Express InterCity Premium, connecting major Polish cities and operating many international services.

name: POLREGIO
description: POLREGIO is Poland's largest regional passenger rail operator, formerly known as Przewozy Regionalne. Its red-and-blue brand appears on local and interregional trains across much of the country, linking smaller towns with regional capitals under public-service contracts.

name: SKM
description: SKM, short for Szybka Kolej Miejska, is a Polish fast urban rail brand used for metropolitan commuter services. SKM trains connect city centers with suburbs on mainline railway infrastructure, with well-known networks in areas such as Warsaw and the Tricity.

name: Koleje Mazowieckie
description: Koleje Mazowieckie is one of Poland's largest regional passenger rail operators, serving Warsaw and the wider Masovian Voivodeship. The KM brand connects the capital with towns such as Radom, Płock, Siedlce, Mława, Sochaczew, and Żyrardów.

name: Koleje Dolnośląskie
description: Koleje Dolnośląskie is the regional passenger rail operator for Poland's Lower Silesian Voivodeship. The KD brand is centered on Wrocław and Legnica, serving dense regional corridors across Dolny Śląsk and cross-border links toward Czechia and Germany.

name: Koleje Małopolskie
description: Koleje Małopolskie is the regional passenger rail operator for Poland's Lesser Poland Voivodeship. The KMŁ brand serves Kraków and surrounding regional corridors, including airport, agglomeration, and interregional services across Małopolska.

name: Koleje Śląskie
description: Koleje Śląskie is the regional passenger rail operator for Poland's Silesian Voivodeship. Its KS brand serves the dense urban and industrial region around Katowice, Gliwice, Częstochowa, Bielsko-Biała, Rybnik, and nearby cross-regional routes.

name: Koleje Wielkopolskie
description: Koleje Wielkopolskie is a regional passenger rail operator owned by the Greater Poland Voivodeship. The KW brand is centered on Poznań and serves routes across Wielkopolska, linking regional towns with the voivodeship capital and neighboring rail networks.

name: Łódzka Kolej Aglomeracyjna
description: Łódzka Kolej Aglomeracyjna is a regional and commuter rail operator serving Łódź and the surrounding Łódź Voivodeship. The ŁKA brand links the metropolitan area with nearby towns and regional centers using modern electric multiple units and integrated regional services.

name: Warszawska Kolej Dojazdowa
description: Warszawska Kolej Dojazdowa is a suburban rail operator serving the south-western Warsaw metropolitan area. The WKD brand runs frequent commuter services from central Warsaw toward Grodzisk Mazowiecki and Milanówek on its own electrified railway line.
